text.title: AI Research Tools Selection Map Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-research-tools methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Tool selection MUST be driven by evidence_type (market_data → Perplexity; scientific_claim → Consensus/Scite; opinion/blog synthesis → NotebookLM; meta-analysis → Elicit).
rule.rationale: Each tool has a corpus + retrieval bias; using the wrong tool produces confident but mis-grounded answers.

rule.statement: When evidence_type=market_data, Perplexity calls MUST set recency_filter=year (or tighter).
rule.rationale: Without recency filter, stale 2022 figures present as current and distort decisions.

rule.statement: Every claim in the output MUST cite a URL or DOI from the tool's source list; uncited claims are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Citation is the only audit trail for AI-mediated research; uncited claims collapse to hallucination risk.

rule.statement: When the question drives a decision worth >$5k, run the query against ≥2 tools and reconcile differences.
rule.rationale: Single-tool answers carry tool-bias risk; reconciliation across tools surfaces disagreement.

rule.statement: If question is open-ended exploration without a research question OR requires systematic review, skip.
rule.rationale: Tool selection assumes a sharp question and time pressure; otherwise alternative approaches dominate.
